title: eriogonum wrightii subsp. trachygonum
intro: Eriogonum wrightii subsp. trachygonum, commonly known as Mormon tea, is a subspecies of the Wright's buckwheat plant, which belongs to the Polygonaceae family. It is a shrub that grows in the southwestern United States and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Mormon tea can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture or infusion for consumption.
